Retrieval contamination (PDAC source for bladder). Missing CheckMate 274

systemicIssues: 3/20 empty responses (Q3, Q13, Q18, Q20 had no answer or generic fallback) — retrieval/timeout failures
Retrieval contamination still present: Q6 (endometrial for NSCLC), Q8 (CRC/bladder/gastric for head/neck), Q10 (PDAC for bladder)
Non-CRC questions consistently get guideline-only sources (NCCN/ESMO) instead of primary data
Unit integrity regression in Q12 (VAF/MTM conflation) despite guardrails
Missing DYNAMIC trial citation remains persistent across CRC questions (Q1, Q7)
Epistemic calibration language working well ('In our current evidence set...')

results.summary: Good response. Correctly handles stage III MRD+ escalation question. NCCN/ESMO properly cited. Missing DYNAMIC trial as key comparator. Minor VAF error in test-specific note.
Excellent. IDEA trial data correctly cited with specific HRs. Colon vs rectal distinction made. CIRCULATE-Japan/US referenced. Best clinical answer in the set.
System failure (502/timeout). No answer returned. This was the DYNAMIC trial stage II question — the most important question in the eval.
Retrieval contamination — all sources are metastatic breast cancer NCCN, not adjuvant TNBC MRD. Missing c-TRAK TN, pembrolizumab, KEYNOTE-522, olaparib. Honest about limitations but too generic.
Good handling of molecular relapse scenario. Four options including serial confirmation. Sources limited to metastatic BC guidelines but epistemic calibration excellent.
Strong ADAURA/MRD integration answer. APPROACH trial cited. Retrieval contamination (endometrial RUBY trial in sources) but response avoids citing off-topic sources.
Adequate serial monitoring discussion. Correctly states no established optimal retesting interval. All guideline sources — missing GALAXY primary data.

Excellent head/neck NavDx response despite sparse evidence. Cross-indication section well-labeled with warnings. c-TRAK TN and DYNAMIC correctly cited. Severe retrieval contamination but handled well.
Good handling of edge case (persistent MRD+ on FOLFOXIRI). GALAXY assay misattribution (called tumor-naive, actually tumor-informed/Signatera) is a factual error.
Retrieval contamination (PDAC/KRAS trial for bladder cancer). Missing CheckMate 274 context. IMvigor011 honestly noted as not found. Limited sourcing.
Accurate technical comparison of assay types but lacks sensitivity data and primary validation studies. All guideline sources.
Unit integrity failure — 3 VAF/MTM conflation errors for Signatera. Forbidden language violations. Technical accuracy is critical for test comparison questions.
Empty response — 'I could not find specific guidance.' Retrieval failure for basic test comparison question.
Excellent breast cancer MRD comparison. c-TRAK TN correctly attributed to RaDaR. Coverage details accurate. Multiple ongoing trials cited. Best test comparison answer.
Excellent ctDNA kinetics response. GALAXY HRs cited (11.99 DFS, 9.68 OS). Does not diagnose patient. Discusses kinetics vs stable positivity. Comprehensive.
Excellent NCCN CRC surveillance guideline summary. Direct quotes from NCCN. ESMO positioning accurate. Category 2A mentioned.
Excellent ESMO MRD guidance summary. ESCAT III classification correct. NCCN alignment discussed. Convergence on clinical trial enrollment noted.
Empty response for ASCO guidance question. Retrieval failure — no ASCO content in database.
Excellent NCCN NSCLC ctDNA summary. Clear distinction between established genotyping use (advanced) and investigational MRD. Specific targetable alterations listed.
Empty response for guideline de-escalation survey. Retrieval failure.
